Fed Cut Odds Surge After Powell's ECB Remarks The odds of a 25 bp rate cut at the Fed's July 30 FOMC meeting jumped to 23.3% from 18.6% yesterday and last week, after Chair Jerome Powell told the ECB forum in Portugal he can't say if July is too early to ease policy.  Markets pushed fed funds futures accordingly, with short-dated contracts repricing a nearly one-in-four chance of a cut.  Powell stressed that any move will be data-dependent, pointing to incoming inflation readings and labor metrics as key drivers.
